free_text: Measurements of 31S energy levels and reevaluation
Tandem Van de Graaff accelerator of
the Yale University's Wright Nuclear Structure
Laboratory and the Enge split-pole magnetic
mu-g/cm2 thickness were made by evaporating
backings supported by aluminum target frames using
at the focal plane backed by a plastic scintillator
to identify tritons corresponding to excited states
in 31S were used to measure their energies.
The Yale Lamp Shade Array (YLSA) of silicon-
strip detectors was mounted at backward angles to
detect coincident proton emission in the
detectors with 16 annular strips apiece. The sectors
cover an angular range from 130deg to 165deg, i.e.  14%
measurement was made by coupling the magnetic-analysis
apparatus to the Yale Lamp Shade Array in order to
detect protons at backward angles in coincidence with
Ratio was set by authors to physical value 1.00
because measured value was greater than unity
